title: Shared Criterion
description: Standalone criterion file in the shared criterion library. Phase 1 commit 1.1 introduces this schema alongside the first SFDR criteria. A framework JSON references a criterion via { ref, weight } in its top-level criteria array; the loader resolves refs against this library. criterion_id is version-stamped (e.g. sfdr_v1_*), independent of the KB-internal label_id namespace.

criterion_id.description: Version-stamped criterion identifier. Format: <regime>_v<n>_<criterion_slug>. Example: sfdr_v1_good_governance_attestation.

regime.description: Regulatory regime + version slug. MUST match the regime portion of criterion_id. Example: sfdr_v1.

regulatory_anchors.description: Primary-source citations. At least one required.

axes.description: LogicInput axes this criterion reads. Matches the engine's InputAxis type from Phase 0.

applies_to.description: Framework ids that may reference this criterion. The validator checks that any framework using { ref: criterion_id } appears in this list.

scoring_status.description: Current scoring implementation state. SFDR commit 1.1 ships criteria with not_implemented; commits 1.2 and 1.3 promote to implemented.

methodology_version_introduced.description: Methodology version in which this criterion was introduced. Example: v3.3.

summary.description: One- to three-sentence summary of what this criterion tests.

successor_regime_note.description: Optional free-text note flagging a pending regulatory rewrite affecting this criterion (e.g. SFDR 2.0 proposal COM(2025) 841).

regulation.description: Human-readable name of the regulation. Example: Regulation (EU) 2019/2088 (SFDR).

celex.description: CELEX identifier of the cited instrument (consolidated variant where applicable). Example: 02019R2088-20240109.

article.description: Article / section reference within the instrument. Example: Article 8(1).

url.description: Optional EUR-Lex (or equivalent) URL for direct access to the primary source.
